decks.notes: This deck focuses on controlling the game by utilizing various counterspells, removal spells, and card draw effects. It aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan while maintaining card advantage and denying them resources. The inclusion of cards like Teferi, Time Raveler and Narset, Parter of Veils help to further control the board and limit the opponent's options. The deck also has a few win conditions such as Monumental Henge and Teferi, Hero of Dominaria to close out the game once it has established control.

This Boros Aggro deck focuses on aggressive creatures and spells to quickly overwhelm the opponent. Utilizing cards like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Seasoned Pyromancer to deal damage and generate value, along with Goblin Bombardment for additional reach. The deck also includes disruption with cards like Ranger-Captain of Eos and Static Prison. Ajani, Nacatl Pariah provides a powerful finisher while Guide of Souls and Voice of Victory offer additional support for the aggro strategy. The mana base is optimized for consistency and speed, allowing for quick and explosive starts.

The deck focuses on aggressive play with a combination of creatures and spells to quickly deal damage to the opponent. It utilizes cards that can generate value through sacrificing creatures, while also including powerful early game threats like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer. The deck also includes disruption in the form of Lightning Bolt and Blood Moon to control the board and pressure the opponent. Overall, the strategy is to apply constant pressure and overwhelm the opponent before they have a chance to stabilize.

This deck focuses on quickly reanimating powerful creatures like Griselbrand using Goryo's Vengeance and Ephemerate while also disrupting the opponent's game plan with counterspells like Force of Negation and hand disruption spells like Thoughtseize. The deck also includes a variety of removal spells to deal with threats on the battlefield. The key strategy is to create explosive plays by combining reanimation spells with powerful creatures, ultimately overwhelming the opponent with a fast and lethal combo.

This Boros Aggro deck focuses on quickly overwhelming the opponent with low-cost aggressive creatures and powerful combat tricks. It is able to put pressure on the opponent early in the game by effectively utilizing cards like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Voice of Victory. The deck also includes some removal spells like Lightning Bolt and Solitude to deal with threats from the opponent. Additionally, the deck utilizes synergies between cards like Ocelot Pride and Goblin Bombardment to keep applying pressure and end the game quickly.

The main focus of this deck is to efficiently cycle through cards and manipulate the graveyard in order to cast Living End and bring back powerful creatures from the graveyard to the battlefield. The deck includes cards like Street Wraith and Shardless Agent to cycle through the deck, as well as Generous Ent to discard cards and Endurance to exile cards from the opponent's graveyard. The combination of Violent Outburst and Living End allows for a powerful board reset that can quickly overwhelm the opponent.

This Mardu Aggro deck focuses on aggressive creature threats combined with burn and removal spells to control the board and push through damage. It also utilizes sacrifice synergies with cards like Goblin Bombardment and Marionette Apprentice to gain incremental advantage. The deck aims to quickly overwhelm the opponent with a mix of versatile creatures and efficient removal, while utilizing cards like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Seasoned Pyromancer for card advantage. The mana base is optimized for consistency and speed, allowing for explosive starts and flexible casting options.

The main theme of this deck is to control the board and disrupt the opponent's strategy through a combination of counterspells, removal, and card advantage. The deck utilizes powerful planeswalkers like Teferi, Hero of Dominaria and Teferi, Time Raveler, as well as efficient removal options like Supreme Verdict and Prismatic Ending. The inclusion of Kaheera, the Orphanguard as a companion adds an additional layer of synergy and versatility to the deck. Overall, the deck aims to outlast the opponent and establish dominance through a mix of control and card advantage.

The deck focuses on utilizing Eldrazi creatures and spell cards to generate powerful combos and overwhelming board presence. It aims to ramp up mana quickly, with cards like Eldrazi Temple and Urza's Saga, to cast powerful Eldrazi creatures like Emrakul, the Promised End. By using support cards such as Blade of the Bloodchief and Malevolent Rumble, the deck can control the board and deal massive damage to the opponent. The inclusion of cards like Writhing Chrysalis and Unholy Heat provides additional versatility and flexibility in dealing with different threats.

The main idea of this deck is to generate a large amount of mana quickly using rituals and mana sources like Ruby Medallion, then chain together spells like Desperate Ritual and Past in Flames to create a storm of spells that eventually culminate in a lethal Grapeshot. Ral, Monsoon Mage helps dig for combo pieces while also providing a win condition with his ultimate ability. The deck aims to win the game by assembling its combo and overwhelming the opponent with a flurry of spells in a single turn.

The deck focuses on utilizing Leyline of the Guildpact and Leyline Binding to control the opponent's board while aggressively attacking with creatures like Territorial Kavu and Scion of Draco. Quantum Riddler and Consign to Memory provide additional control and card advantage. Lightning Bolt offers versatile removal, while Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Phelia, Exuberant Shepherd provide efficient threats. Stubborn Denial offers protection for your key cards. Teferi, Time Raveler disrupts the opponent's strategy, and Arena of Glory and Overlord of the Balemurk provide additional value for your creatures. The mana base is optimized for consistency and flexibility.

The deck focuses on assembling a powerful combo involving Basking Broodscale, Glaring Fleshraker, and other support cards to generate a massive amount of damage quickly. The deck utilizes Eldrazi Temple and other mana sources to ramp into powerful threats like Emrakul, the Promised End and Walking Ballista. Kozilek's Command and Malevolent Rumble provide removal and disruption, while Blade of the Bloodchief buffs up creatures for a lethal assault. Ancient Stirrings and Urza's Saga help find key pieces of the combo, making the deck a formidable force in Modern 1 vs 1 play.

This deck focuses on controlling the game by countering spells, removing threats, and manipulating the game state with cards like Teferi, Time Raveler and Isochron Scepter. The deck also includes cards like Prismatic Ending and Solitude for removal, while utilizing Consule the Star Charts and Tune the Narrative for card advantage and selection. The addition of Kaheera, the Orphanguard as a companion provides additional value and synergy with the deck's strategy. Overall, the key concept of this UW Control deck is to outlast and outmaneuver opponents through careful resource management and strategic decision-making.

This control deck revolves around assembling the UrzaTron lands (Urza's Tower, Power Plant, and Mine) to generate large amounts of mana quickly. The deck focuses on deploying powerful Eldrazi creatures and planeswalkers like Karn, the Great Creator and Ugin, the Eye of the Storms to control the board and overwhelm the opponent. It also includes disruption in the form of Chalice of the Void and Thought-Knot Seer to disrupt the opponent's game plan. The goal is to establish dominance through mana advantage and powerful threats to secure victory.

The deck focuses on utilizing card draw and counter spells to protect its combo pieces, with a win condition centered around Goblin Charbelcher to deal massive damage to the opponent. By leveraging cards like Hydroelectric Specimen, Lotus Bloom, and Tameshi, Reality Architect, the deck aims to quickly assemble the necessary pieces for a game-winning combo without relying on traditional land-based resources. The inclusion of disruptive spells like Sink into Stupor and Suppression Ray further strengthens the deck's ability to control the game and ensure the combo can be executed successfully.

The main concept of this Boros Ponza control deck is to disrupt the opponent's mana base and resources by destroying their lands with cards like Cleansing Wildfire and Field of Ruin, while also controlling the board with removal spells like Erode and Wrath of God. The deck also utilizes powerful planeswalkers like Karn, the Great Creator and card advantage engines like Tablet of Discovery to outvalue the opponent. Additionally, the inclusion of Kaheera, the Orphanguard as a companion provides an additional creature to support the control strategy.
